title: nematolepis squamea subsp. squamea
intro: Nematolepis squamea subsp. squamea, also known as a shrub or small tree native to Australia, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Nematolepis squamea subsp. squamea include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin conditions, respiratory issues, and wound healing. In traditional Australian herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or oil by boiling the leaves and stems in water or extracting the essential oils through steam distillation.
